title: my ai seo saas just launched to 3k mrr with zero paid ads
raw_text: My AI SEO SaaS Just Launched to $3k MRR With Zero Paid Ads - Indie Hackers Home Starting Up Case Studies DB Products Ideas DB Vibe Coding Tools Subscribe to IH+ Starting Up Case Studies Ideas DB Products DB Join 0 Likes -1 Bookmarks 2 Comments My AI SEO SaaS Just Launched to $3k MRR With Zero Paid Ads IH+ Subscribers Only By Mark Gadala October 29, 2023 I recently launched my new SaaS product that uses AI to do the entire SEO process called Post Cheetah. I launched in late August and by the end of September we were already a little over $3k MRR, with zero paid ads. I've been doing SEO for over 12 years and when I started finally using ChatGPT late last year I knew I had to start building something amazing to help do the entire SEO process using AI. I'm extremely proud of what we have built and I have a TON of new awesome features on the way to solidify us as the go to AI SEO tool on the market. How It Started January 2023 I basically came up with this idea in January of this year and started working on it immediately, since I already run an SEO agency it didn't matter to me if I found "product market fit." I was solving my own problem and it was going to make my life way easier. Initial Marketing Journey March–August 2023 I realized quickly that it's very very hard to get attention onto a new SaaS unless you have endless marketing resources, which I don't. I tried running some Facebook ads to an early access sign up page with "mixed" results. Since it was going to take way too much money (& time) I had to find another way. So I started building up my Twitter following, AI on Twitter was going crazy so I decided to jump into the "thread boi" trend and start posting informative and fun threads on how to use AI (using methods I was learning/studying). The results were insane. I grew 45k followers in 3 months, I got 7,500 people on my early access list, and I got around 6800 people signed up to my personal newsletter. Launching The Product September–October 2023 I started to message my list 100 contacts at a time to let them know early sign ups were ready. This brought on our first users and I was able to get a TON of bugs worked out and some very helpful feedback which helped us tailor it a lot. I finished emailing everyone on the list at the end of September and by then we had already reached $3k MRR. Next Steps & How I Plan To 100x MRR Since I'm nowhere near my goal and I know just how massive the potential is for this product I'm working on taking the next steps to reach it's full potential. My Current Challenges: Working on a sustainable and predictable marketing channel that won't rely on my email list Working on a WAY better customer retention and onboarding process (churn is way too high currently) Focusing on adding features more efficiently & focusing on the right things to add so I don't waste time. I hope this helped inspire some fellow Indie Hackers and I'm always open for a chat if anyone wants to reach out. Also if you have any suggestions for improvement or scalable marketing I'd love to hear them. I experimented with many different email styles at the beginning with small samples sizes and found that the most direct emails worked "You're Free Early Access to Post Cheetah is Ready!" - With a huge "CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP!" button at to top of the body Mark Gadala · 3 years ago · Reply 1 Congratulations on the successful launch of your AI SEO SaaS (Search Engine Optimization Software as a Service) and achieving $3,000 in Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR) without using paid advertising! This is a significant milestone, and it reflects the quality of your product and your ability to attract and retain customers. Here are a few considerations as you continue to grow your SaaS business: Customer Feedback: Continue to actively collect and analyze feedback from your customers. Use this valuable information to make improvements and enhancements to your SaaS platform. Content Marketing: Consider creating high-quality, SEO-optimized content to attract organic traffic to your website. This can help you reach potential customers without relying on paid ads. Referral and Affiliate Programs: Encourage your existing customers to refer new customers to your SaaS platform. You can also explore setting up an affiliate program to reward others for promoting your service. Content Strategy: Develop a content strategy that showcases the value and benefits of your AI SEO SaaS. This can include blog posts, case studies, webinars, and video content. Email Marketing: Utilize email marketing to nurture leads and engage with your current customers. Send product updates, industry insights, and helpful tips. Customer Success: Focus on providing excellent customer service and support to retain existing customers and build long-term relationships. Scaling Marketing Efforts: If you decide to invest in paid advertising in the future, make sure to carefully plan and track the ROI to ensure it's a profitable investment. Community Building: Consider creating a community or forum for users to share experiences, best practices, and ask questions related to your SaaS platform. Analyze Data: Use data and analytics tools to understand user behavior, track conversions, and optimize your marketing efforts. Stay Agile: Be open to adjusting your strategy as you learn more about your market and customers. The ability to adapt is crucial for long-term success. Scaling and Scaling Resources: As your MRR continues to grow, be prepared to scale your resources and team to support increased demand and provide a seamless user experience. The fact that you've reached $3,000 MRR without paid advertising suggests that there's a demand for your product and that you're doing well in attracting and retaining customers. Keep building on your success and adapting to market dynamics, and your AI SEO SaaS business can continue to thrive.
